source_jobs.title: Director of Growth, AvaCloud
source_jobs.description:
Looking to join a world-class blockchain development team? Ava Labs makes it simple to deploy high-performance solutions for Web3, led by innovations on Avalanche. The company was founded by Cornell computer scientists, who partnered with Wall Street veterans and early Web3 leaders to execute a promising vision for redefining the way people build permissionless networks. Ava Labs is redefining the way people create value with Web3. Join us as we empower people to easily and freely digitize all the world’s assets on one open, programmable blockchain platform.

AvaCloud is experiencing incredible growth and is seeking a Director of Growth to accelerate adoption.  Reporting to the CEO, AvaCloud, this is an exciting opportunity to lead our global growth strategy.  This is an ideal role for professionals with a strong business entrepreneurial mindset who have prior experience selling SaaS products, and driving business development and partnerships.

WHAT YOU WILL DO

 - Develop and execute growth strategies: Create and implement data-driven strategies to drive customer acquisition, activation, retention, and revenue growth.

 - Lead growth experiments: Design and conduct A/B tests, analyze results, and iterate on marketing campaigns and product features to optimize key metrics.

 - Collaborate across teams: Work closely with marketing, product, sales, and business development teams to align growth initiatives with overall business objectives.

 - Analyze data and identify opportunities: Utilize data analytics to understand user behavior, identify growth opportunities, and measure the effectiveness of campaigns.

 - Manage growth budget and resources: Allocate resources effectively to maximize ROI and achieve growth targets.

 - Stay ahead of the curve: Research and implement new growth tactics, tools, and technologies to maintain a competitive edge.

 - Build a growth-oriented culture: Foster a data-driven, experimental mindset across the organization and empower teams to contribute to growth initiatives.

 - Report on growth performance: Track and analyze key growth metrics, communicate progress to stakeholders, and provide actionable insights.

WHAT YOU WILL BRING

 - Bachelors Degree or MBA

 - Proven track record: Demonstrated success in driving significant growth in a SaaS, PaaS or Web3 technology company.

 - Data-driven mindset: Strong analytical skills with the ability to extract insights from data and make data-informed decisions.

 - Growth hacking expertise: Deep understanding of growth hacking principles, A/B testing methodologies, and user acquisition strategies.

 - Marketing and product knowledge: Familiarity with various marketing channels (SEO, SEM, social media, content marketing) and product development processes.

 - Leadership and communication skills: Ability to lead and inspire cross-functional teams, communicate effectively with stakeholders, and build consensus.

 - Creative problem-solving: A knack for identifying creative solutions to growth challenges and a willingness to experiment and take risks.

 - Passion for growth: A genuine enthusiasm for driving growth and a commitment to continuous learning and improvement.

 - Technical proficiency: Comfortable with data analysis tools, marketing automation platforms, and other relevant technologies.

(**This is not a guarantee of compensation or salary, a final offer amount may vary based on factors including but not limited to experience and geographic location.)

WHY AVA LABS?

If you've ever thought about joining an early stage Web3 company - this is it! We're a global, world-class team of experts in computer science, economics, finance, marketing, and law with offices in New York City and Miami. We're highly passionate about Web3 and redefining the way people build and use finance and decentralized applications of all kinds. The company received early-stage funding from Andreessen Horowitz, Initialized Capital, and Polychain Capital, with angel investment

source_jobs.title: Director of Social Media
source_jobs.description:
Looking to join a world-class blockchain development team? Ava Labs makes it simple to deploy high-performance solutions for Web3, led by innovations on Avalanche. The company was founded by Cornell computer scientists, who partnered with Wall Street veterans and early Web3 leaders to execute a promising vision for redefining the way people build permissionless networks. Ava Labs is redefining the way people create value with Web3. Join us as we empower people to easily and freely digitize all the world’s assets on one open, programmable blockchain platform.

Director of Social Media
Location: East Coast (NYC Preferred)

We are looking for an exceptional Director of Social Media to lead Avalanche’s brand presence across all owned channels and shape how the world perceives Avalanche. This is a senior individual contributor and people management role that sits within the Marketing organization, reporting to the SVP of Marketing.

The ideal candidate brings a rare combination: deep hands-on experience in crypto and institutional marketing alongside proven experience communicating to institutional audiences such as financial services, fintech, or enterprise technology. You understand how to speak credibly to both the DeFi power user and the institutional investor — and you can craft content that resonates with both.

This role requires someone who is highly creative, self-sufficient with production tools, and passionate about building in public. You will own our voice and move fast.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES


CHANNEL OWNERSHIP & STRATEGY

 - Own and drive strategy for all Ava Labs social channels: Twitter/X, LinkedIn, YouTube, TikTok, and Instagram

 - Develop and execute platform-specific content calendars aligned to product launches, ecosystem milestones, and market moments

 - Grow audience, engagement, and brand affinity across all platforms with a data-driven approach


EXECUTIVE SOCIAL MEDIA SUPPORT

 - Proactively surface timely tweet recommendations and reactive commentary opportunities aligned with industry conversations


TEAM LEADERSHIP

 - Directly manage and mentor a team of 1, setting clear goals and career development priorities

 - Serve as the senior social voice within the Marketing org, collaborating cross-functionally with PR/Communications, Content, Partner Marketing, Events, and BD teams

 - Establish scalable processes for content production, approval, scheduling, and performance reporting


CONTENT PRODUCTION

 - Independently clip, edit, and publish short-form video content from long-form sources (podcasts, AMAs, interviews, events)

 - Perform basic photo editing and graphic production to enable fast publishing without always requiring designer support

 - Source and adapt content from across the Avalanche ecosystem to amplify community voices and partner narratives


ANALYTICS & REPORTING

 - Track, analyze, and report on channel performance metrics; translate insights into actionable strategy adjustments

 - Monitor competitor and industry social activity to identify trends and positioning opportunities


REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - 7+ years of social media experience, with at least 3 years in a senior leadership role within crypto, Web3, or DeFi

 - Demonstrable experience at a crypto-native or crypto-adjacent institutional platform (e.g., FalconX, Coinbase, Kraken, Grayscale, Gemini, or comparable)

 - Expert-level fluency across Twitter/X, LinkedIn, YouTube, TikTok, and Instagram — including algorithm dynamics and platform best practices

 - Hands-on video editing skills: comfortable clipping long-form content and producing short-form social assets independently (CapCut, Adobe Premiere, or similar)

 - Working proficiency in Adobe Photoshop or equivalent for basic graphic production without relying on a design team

 - Strong written communication skills with the ability to adapt tone, style, and format across audiences and platforms

 - Based on the East Coast; NYC preferred for in-person collaborat

source_jobs.title: Senior Forward Deployed Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Looking to join a world-class blockchain development team? Ava Labs makes it simple to deploy high-performance solutions for Web3, led by innovations on Avalanche. The company was founded by Cornell computer scientists, who partnered with Wall Street veterans and early Web3 leaders to execute a promising vision for redefining the way people build permissionless networks. Ava Labs is redefining the way people create value with Web3. Join us as we empower people to easily and freely digitize all the world’s assets on one open, programmable blockchain platform.

Looking to join a world-class blockchain development team? Ava Labs makes it simple to deploy high-performance solutions for Web3, led by innovations on Avalanche. The company was founded by Cornell computer scientists, who partnered with Wall Street veterans and early Web3 leaders to execute a promising vision for redefining the way people build permissionless networks. Ava Labs is redefining the way people create value with Web3. Join us as we empower people to easily and freely digitize all the world's assets on one open, programmable blockchain platform.



We are looking for a Senior Forward Deployed Engineer to join the Forward-Deployed Engineering (FDE) function at Ava Labs. This is a senior individual contributor role sitting within the Platform Engineering Group, reporting to the Head of Forward Deployed Engineering.

FDE is a partner-facing engineering team that designs and ships bespoke solutions for a small set of high-profile regulated financial institutions deploying on Avalanche and also owns key portions of the developer tooling and SDK surface that those partners (and the broader ecosystem) build against. The team operates through deep technical engagements, not back-to-back calls: weeks of hands-on engineering between syncs is the norm.

The right person brings Avalanche stack fluency, hands-on enterprise/on-prem experience, and AI-native execution, and can translate a Director of Engineering's requirements at a regulated financial institution into a working solution without a handoff chain.


WHAT YOU WILL DO

Partner Delivery

 - Own FDE technical delivery across a named set of institutional partners — designing the architecture across the entire Avalanche stack (node / validator setup, failover procedures, interoperability, …) from partner ask to working output

 - Pair with Enterprise Go-to-Market on active POCs to close the gap between commercial requirements and production-grade technical solutions

 - Represent Avalanche credibly in partner-facing technical settings: sit across from Directors of Engineering at financial institutions and translate bespoke asks into scoped requirements without losing the strategic frame

 - Engage in depth, not volume — most weeks are heads-down implementation work punctuated by focused technical syncs, not a meeting-heavy customer-success cadence

Protocol & Infrastructure

 - Work hands-on across the Avalanche stack: AvalancheGo, EVM, Interchain Messaging, and validator infrastructure

 - Design and implement solutions that operate within the constraints regulated institutions actually run: KMS/HSM remote signing, audit trails, restricted-peer networking, backup/restore procedures, firewall topologies

 - Respond to security assessment requests and technical diligence from institutional partners

Tooling & Developer Infrastructure

 - Contribute to and maintain the developer tooling and SDK surface owned by FDE. This is the same tooling that underpins partner deployments and the broader Avalanche ecosystem

 - Harden, extend, and document tooling driven by recurring institutional requirements, so that the next partner inherits a more capable platform than the last

 - Treat tooling work and partner delivery as a single loop: lessons from each engagement land back in the shared surface

Playbook Development

 - Identify when a one-off partner solution should become a reusable FDE artifact and drive that abstraction

source_jobs.title: Senior Product Manager, Fortary
source_jobs.description:
Looking to join a world-class blockchain development team? Ava Labs makes it simple to deploy high-performance solutions for Web3, led by innovations on Avalanche. The company was founded by Cornell computer scientists, who partnered with Wall Street veterans and early Web3 leaders to execute a promising vision for redefining the way people build permissionless networks. Ava Labs is redefining the way people create value with Web3. Join us as we empower people to easily and freely digitize all the world’s assets on one open, programmable blockchain platform.

We’re looking for a Senior Manager - Product to lead the strategy and execution of Fortary’s institutional platform across trading, lending, and liquidity. This leader will define the long-term vision at the intersection of product, markets, and infrastructure—focused on building best-in-class digital asset solutions for sophisticated financial institutions.

You'll define the long-term product vision, strategy, and investment priorities across Fortary’s institutional trading, lending, and liquidity offerings. Working closely with engineering, design, business development, and executive leadership, you'll align product investments with customer needs, market opportunities, regulatory requirements, and business objectives to drive sustainable growth. This role requires deep expertise in institutional market structure and workflows, exceptional strategic judgment, and the ability to translate complex market dynamics into scalable products and measurable business outcomes.


The ideal candidate brings a proven track record of defining and scaling successful B2B fintech or digital asset products, shaping long-term product strategy, and leading complex cross-functional initiatives. They thrive in fast-paced, high-growth environments, combining strategic thinking with strong execution and the ability to influence senior stakeholders across the organization. In this role, you'll help define Fortary's institutional product strategy, guide investment decisions across key product areas, and drive adoption and commercial success with institutional clients globally.



WHAT YOU WILL DO

 - As the business architect for trading, lending, and liquidity, the Senior Product Manager will define and lead the long-term product vision, portfolio strategy, and investment priorities for Fortary's institutional trading, lending, and liquidity platform, ensuring alignment with company objectives and growth strategy. The plan will consider a build, buy, partner approach to enrich these capabilities across the Fortary Roadmap.

 - Partner with executive leadership to shape product strategy, prioritize investments, and drive strategic initiatives that accelerate Fortary's institutional business

 - Execute cross-functionally with engineering, business development, marketing, and design to deliver measurable customer and business outcomes

 - Develop deep insights into institutional client needs, market structure, and competitive dynamics to identify new market opportunities and inform product strategy

 - Build trusted relationships with institutional clients and strategic partners to validate product direction, strengthen adoption, and identify new growth opportunities

 - Own product commercialization in partnership with executive leadership and business development, including go-to-market strategy, product positioning, pricing considerations, and successful launches

 - Define and benchmark institutional competitors with a focus on institutional trading, lending, and liquidity to differentiate Fortary with it’s clients

 - Develop and scale core platform capabilities across Fortary, ensuring a seamless, secure, and high-quality user experience

 - Drive prioritization and investment decisions across multiple product initiatives, balancing customer value, technical complexity, regulatory considerations, and commercial impact

 - Drive the development and scaling of secure, reliable, and high-perf

source_jobs.title: Executive Assistant
source_jobs.description:
Looking to join a world-class blockchain development team? Ava Labs makes it simple to deploy high-performance solutions for Web3, led by innovations on Avalanche. The company was founded by Cornell computer scientists, who partnered with Wall Street veterans and early Web3 leaders to execute a promising vision for redefining the way people build permissionless networks. Ava Labs is redefining the way people create value with Web3. Join us as we empower people to easily and freely digitize all the world’s assets on one open, programmable blockchain platform.

Executive Assistant - New York, NY

Ava Labs is the team behind Avalanche, one of the most performant and widely adopted Layer 1 blockchain networks in the world. Our executive team operates at the intersection of deep technical innovation, institutional capital, and global policy.

We are seeking a highly accomplished and discreet Executive Assistant to provide dedicated, high-touch support to our CEO, President and COO. Operating at the centre of the organization's most critical decisions and relationships, this role demands exceptional judgement, precision, and the ability to work with a high degree of autonomy in a fast-paced environment.

The ideal candidate is a strategic partner who proactively anticipates needs, manages complexity with grace, and serves as a trusted extension of the executive team.




ABOUT THE ROLE




EXECUTIVE CALENDAR AND SCHEDULE MANAGEMENT

 - Own and orchestrate complex, high-priority calendars for three C-suite principals simultaneously, resolving conflicts with sound judgement and minimal direction.

 - Strategically protect executives time by prioritizing meetings, flagging scheduling conflicts, and ensuring alignment with organizational priorities and leadership rhythms.

 - Coordinate and manage all scheduling for board meetings, leadership off-sites, all-hands events, and executive steering committees.

 - Proactively prepare daily and weekly briefings, ensuring all principals are fully informed and prepared for every engagement.


EXECUTIVE COMMUNICATIONS AND CORRESPONDENCE

 - Serve as the primary communication hub between the CEO, President, COO and internal and external stakeholders, triaging inboxes, drafting high-quality correspondence, and ensuring timely follow-through.

 - Demonstrate mature judgment in all communications with and on behalf of executives, knowing when to engage, when to hold back, and how to protect executive headspace during high-intensity periods.

 - Exercise considerable discretion in managing sensitive and confidential communications on behalf of all principals.

 - Prepare briefing documents, meeting agendas, talking points, and follow-up summaries for key engagements, including QBRs and company-wide communications from the CEO, President and COO.


TRAVEL AND LOGISTICS

 - Plan and execute complex, multi-leg domestic and international travel itineraries including private and commercial air, ground transportation, accommodations, and visa and passport logistics.

 - Anticipate and resolve travel disruptions in real time, ensuring executives are never without a contingency plan.

 - Coordinate closely with executive protection personnel, security teams, and home-office staff on travel planning with full awareness of security requirements.

 - Manage detailed travel budgets and reconcile expenses in accordance with company policy.


STRATEGIC AND OPERATIONAL SUPPORT

 - Support the development of QBRs, board materials, and company-wide executive communications, including research, synthesis, deck preparation, and follow-up tracking.

 - Manage sensitive visitor and guest protocols, including access management, NDA routing, and escort coordination.

 - Build and maintain strong relationships with home-office staff, senior executive assistants, and department heads to ensure seamless information flow and operational alignment.

 - Lead or assist with special projects and strategic initiatives as assigned b

notes: Blockchain development team with a focus on Web3 and Avalanche.
